# Context Card Format

> Format specification for context cards generated by the Context Agent.
> Source: ONE-Ontology.md Context dimension, NexusModelOverview.md
> A context card is not a document — it is a compact, scoped briefing.
> It arrives once, before the Task Performer calls any other tool.

---

## Purpose

The context card answers: *does the agent have what it needs to do this work well?*

Empirical finding (ONE Ontology, Context dimension): giving an agent more instructions
does not improve performance — it degrades it. The context card exists to deliver only
the relevant subset of available knowledge, not all of it.

---

## Format

```markdown
# Context Card — {task_id}

**Task:** {title}
**State:** {current_state}
**Generated:** {timestamp}

## What this task requires
{1–3 sentences drawn from the task spec — the core problem being solved}

## Relevant patterns from knowledge base
{Only patterns tagged with this task's domain and task_type. Maximum 3 entries.}
- [{source_session}] {pattern_text}

## Sprint learnings that apply
{Only learnings where task_type matches. Maximum 3 entries.}
- [{source_session}] {learning_text}

## Known risks or constraints
{Drawn from uncertainty log for this task or closely related tasks. Maximum 2 entries.}
- {risk_text}

## What you should NOT do
{Only if a past QAFAILED on this task_type produced a specific anti-pattern. Maximum 2.}
- {anti_pattern_text}
```

---

## Token Budget

| Section | Maximum tokens |
|---|---|
| What this task requires | 100 |
| Relevant patterns | 150 (3 × 50 each) |
| Sprint learnings | 150 (3 × 50 each) |
| Known risks | 80 |
| What you should NOT do | 80 |
| Headers and metadata | 60 |
| **Total** | **~620 tokens** |

**Hard rule: never exceed 800 tokens.** A context card that exceeds this has not been compressed — it has been dumped. Return to the knowledge base queries and filter more aggressively.

---

## What to Include vs. Exclude

**Include:**
- Facts specific to this task_type and domain combination
- Patterns with high reuse frequency (high `usage_count` in knowledge base)
- Learnings from tasks that share the same stack component or integration point
- Anti-patterns that caused QAFAILED on closely related tasks

**Exclude:**
- Full task specs (the agent reads this via `read_task_spec_{slug}`)
- Feature specs, acceptance criteria, decomposition docs (too broad)
- Learnings not tagged with a matching domain
- Patterns with usage_count = 1 (too specific to be general)
- Any content the agent can retrieve via a tool call — don't pre-load tool outputs

---

## Phase 1–5 Minimum vs. Target

| Phase | `get_context_card` returns | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Phase 1–5 (experiment) | Raw task row from `SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE id = ?` | ✅ Accepted minimum |
| Post-adoption (target) | Generated context card following this format | Target when Context Agent (2.3) is built |

The Phase 1–5 minimum means agents get the task title and current state — enough to begin.
The target format means agents get a curated briefing drawn from accumulated framework knowledge.

---

## Validation Checklist (for the Context Agent)

Before emitting a context card, verify:

| # | Check | Pass condition |
|---|---|---|
| V1 | Token count | Under 800 tokens |
| V2 | All entries are filtered to this task's domain and task_type | No generic advice — all content is specific |
| V3 | No full document is included | Task spec and feature spec are excluded — only excerpts where needed |
| V4 | "What you should NOT do" has evidence | Only included if a QAFAILED record exists — never invented |
| V5 | Knowledge base entries include `source_session` | Caller can locate the origin — no anonymous claims |
